author: anon-a7c6025f
content:
Decision: Taking a pragmatic approach. Rather than restructuring the entire Conv2d module, I'll:
1. Add GPU dispatch to Conv2d::forward — when input is CUDA f32, call gpu_conv2d_f32
2. For backward: save the input tensor (on GPU) instead of cols. In backward, recompute im2col on GPU (same as PyTorch's memory-efficient mode)
3. The backward grad_weight and grad_input computations use GPU matmul directly

This avoids the massive refactor while getting the key performance win: keeping tensors on GPU through forward and backward.
